item.title: Discrete Math Ch02 Logic of Compound Statements
item.description:
This is the reading note for Chapter 02 of the book *Discrete Mathematic with Applications*.
Chapter 02 "The Logic of Compound Statements" introduces the basics of **propositional logic**. It explores how *statements* are combined using *logical connectives*, explains different forms of *conditional statements*, and discusses *valid arguments* and *common fallacies*.

item.title: Discrete Math Ch01 Speaking Mathematically
item.description:
This is the reading note for Chapter 01 of the book *Discrete Mathematic with Applications*.
Chapter 01 "Speaking Mathematically" introduces foundation for mathematical thought, the language of *variables*, *sets*, *relations*, and *functions*.

item.title: Spring Framework
item.description: ***Spring Framework*** is a Java platform that provides comprehensive *infrastructure support* for developing Java applications.

item.title: Maven
item.description: ***Maven*** is a *project management tool* that is based on *POM* (*project object model*). It is used for **projects build**, **dependency** and **documentation**.

item.title: Docker
item.description: ***Docker*** is a platform for *developing*, *shipping*, and *deploying* applications quickly in **portable, self-sufficient containers**, and is used in the **Continuous Deployment (CD)** stage of the **DevOps** ecosystem.

item.title: PuTTY with OpenSSH
item.description: Today we use ***OpenSSH*** and ***PuTTY*** to log in remote computers.

item.title: MIT 6.033 CSE Security
item.description: Section ***Security*** of MIT 6.033 shows ***common pitfalls*** in the security of computer systems, and ways to *combat* them.

item.title: MIT 6.033 CSE Distributed Systems
item.description: Section ***Distributed Systems*** of MIT 6.033 covers: how ***reliable, usable distributed systems*** are able to be built on top of an *unreliable* network.

item.title: MIT 6.033 CSE Networking
item.description: Section ***Networking*** of MIT 6.033: how the ***Internet*** is designed to *scale* and its various applications.

item.title: MIT 6.033 CSE Operating System
item.description: Section ***Operating Systems*** of MIT 6.033 covers common *design patterns* to limit *complexity* and techniques, like *virtualization* and *abstraction*, to enforce *modularity*.

item.title: Linked List
item.description: A Linked List is a *data structure* that stores data into a series of *connected nodes*, and thus it can be dynamically allocated.

item.title: Servlet
item.description: A *Servlet* is a *Java* programming language *class*, which is executed in *Web Server* and responsible for *dynamic* content generation in a portable way. *Servlet* extends the capabilities of servers that host applications accessed by means of a *request-response programming model*.

item.title: How to Sign Our Git Commits with GPG
item.description: In this post, we talk about how to use GPG to sign a Git commit.

item.title: DDIA Ch01: Reliable, Scalable, and Maintainable Applications
item.description: *Chapter 01: Reliable, Scalable, and Maintainable Applications* of **Designing Data-Intensive Applications**

item.title: Binary Tree NonRecursive InOrder
item.description:
Hi there, let's talk about how to nonrecursively do a In-Order traversal for a Binary Tree.

A Binary Tree consists of 3 parts: the node itself, pointer to the left child, pointer to the right child.

An In-Order Traversal is to access the leftmost child firstly, then the node itself, and finally the right child.

item.title: Hello World
item.description: How to use Hugo to build a personal blog, and publish it onto GitHub Pages.
